title: Essential Tremor
chief_complaint: My hands shake when I try to do things. I can't write checks or drink coffee without spilling. It gets better when I have a glass of wine. My father had the same thing.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Bilateral action tremor (postural and kinetic) of both hands. No rest tremor. No rigidity, bradykinesia, or postural instability. No head tremor. No voice tremor. Normal gait. Normal cerebellar testing.
physical_exam.Writing: Large, tremulous handwriting (not micrographia). Archimedes spiral shows tremor.

hidden_data.mri_brain: Normal. No cerebellar atrophy. No basal ganglia abnormalities.

correct_diagnosis: Essential Tremor (Bilateral Upper Extremity Action Tremor)
key_learning_points: Essential tremor = most common movement disorder. Bilateral action tremor (postural + kinetic). Family history in 50%
Differentiate from Parkinson: ET = action tremor, bilateral, no rigidity/bradykinesia, alcohol improves tremor. PD = rest tremor, unilateral onset, rigidity, bradykinesia, alcohol has no effect
Treatment: Propranolol 20-80mg or Primidone 50-250mg (first-line). Gabapentin or Topiramate (second-line). DBS of VIM thalamus for refractory cases
Alcohol temporarily reduces tremor in 50-70% of ET patients (diagnostic clue but NOT a treatment recommendation)
